//includeTags: M8.4 generates ONLY the routes it actually runs through. That is not merely scope discipline -- it is
the only way src/app/api/** can be trusted.
These five tags are exactly the 15 routes whose C# declares a response type (.Produces<T>()). The
other 64 routes carry the default assembly tag 'TimesheetApp.Api' and declare NO response body at all,
because they return `IResult` via `Results.Ok(x)` and ApiExplorer cannot infer a schema from that.
Generating them would emit methods typed `void` for endpoints that in fact return data -- a client that
lies, committed to the repo, that a later wave would reasonably trust.
So a route joins this list when, and only when, its C# gains a .Produces<T>(). Annotate first, then
generate. Never widen this list to 'fix' a missing method.
//ignoreUnusedModels: Keeps the emitted model set to what the included operations actually reference.
